description_long: This object is a compact, semi-automatic pistol-style firearm with a distinctive long, slender barrel and a rounded trigger guard. The frame and slide are constructed from dark, blued metal, showing signs of wear and age, while the grip panels are ribbed and appear to be made of dark brown wood or bakelite. The top of the receiver is flat, with a rear sight notch and a front blade sight visible. The magazine is internal and not externally protruding, and the overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century military sidearms. The muzzle end is threaded, possibly for the attachment of a suppressor or other muzzle device, but currently only a thread protector is present. The firearm's compact size and ergonomic grip suggest it was designed for one-handed operation and easy concealment.
description: A compact, vintage pistol-style firearm with a long barrel, ribbed dark grip, and blued metal finish. The design features a threaded muzzle and internal magazine.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the barrel, ribbed grip, and trigger guard.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the flat receiver, rear sight notch, and barrel alignment.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the grip texture and slide details.
description_view_3: Front-on view emphasizing the barrel's round profile and the narrow width of the firearm.
